# SaaS Device Principal

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/configreference -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to Daemon Config Reference](../config-reference.md)

When `RIIDO_SAAS_URL` is selected with `RIIDO_DEVICE_ID` and `RIIDO_DEVICE_SECRET`, `saasplane` uses DevicePrincipal authentication.

The daemon reports runtime snapshots to `/v1/daemon/runtime-snapshot`, polls `/v1/daemon/agent-bindings`, then polls the agent-specific assignment endpoint.

Legacy `RIIDO_SAAS_AGENTS` and `RIIDO_SAAS_TOKEN` are not read by the daemon settings model.

If `RIIDO_DAEMON_ID` is unset on this path, daemon id defaults to `RIIDO_DEVICE_ID`. This keeps runtime ids such as `device-enrolled-000123:codex` scoped to the enrolled device instead of `agentd-local:codex`.

Desktop first enrolls the device through the logged-in webview session, then launches daemon with `RIIDO_DEVICE_ID` and `RIIDO_DEVICE_SECRET`. `saasplane` sends them as `X-Riido-Device-ID` and `X-Riido-Device-Secret`.

Team IDs, Open API keys, task-context paths, and workspace API keys are not identity bridges.
